response.justification: Candidate is a strong full-stack software craftsman with web, mobile, and embedded experience, but the role requires deep specialization in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware, deep learning workload optimization, and hardware-software co-design. The resume shows no evidence of GPU architecture work, SIMD/MIMD experience, DNN training/inference optimization, or any hardware modeling background. The closest adjacent experience is low-latency DLang services and some C/C++ work on a measurement IDE, which does not meet the bar for a Principal Performance Architect on AI accelerator infrastructure.
strengths: Strong polyglot engineering background with C/C++ exposure at National Instruments
Demonstrated low-latency systems work handling millions of requests/second
Long track record of technical leadership, teaching, and open-source contribution
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, or chip architecture experience
No AI accelerator, GPU architecture, or DNN training/inference optimization background
Educational and experience profile is software-focused, not aligned with a Principal hardware performance architect role

key_factors.factor: Lack of SoC/IP modeling and hardware architecture experience
No AI accelerator or GPU workload optimization background
Solid systems and low-latency software engineering foundation
